rbi_directives.title: Master Direction on Digital Payment Security Controls
rbi_directives.summary: RBI mandates all regulated entities (banks, payment aggregators) to implement multi-factor authentication for digital payments. OTP via SMS is the primary second factor. Banks must detect and report any malware that intercepts OTPs within 6 hours of detection.
key_controls: MFA mandatory for all transactions above INR 5000
OTP validity must not exceed 30 seconds for high-value transactions
Banks must implement transaction anomaly detection
Mobile banking apps must detect rooted/jailbroken devices
Certificate pinning mandatory for mobile banking apps
In-app overlay detection recommended for Android apps
rbi_directives.penalty: Violation may result in fine up to INR 1 crore per incident and suspension of payment services

rbi_directives.title: IT Framework for Banks — Cyber Security Policy
rbi_directives.summary: RBI mandates banks to maintain a 24x7 SOC, conduct threat intelligence sharing, and implement mobile application security testing before deployment. Third-party APKs used for banking must undergo security review.
key_controls: 24x7 SOC mandatory for scheduled commercial banks
VAPT of mobile banking apps before release
Threat intelligence sharing with CERT-In and RBI-CSITE
Incident reporting within 2 hours of detection
Customer advisory within 24 hours of confirmed fraud
Malware analysis capability or third-party empanelment
rbi_directives.penalty: Non-compliance reported to Board; RBI inspection findings

rbi_directives.title: Customer Protection Guidelines — Mobile Banking Fraud
rbi_directives.summary: RBI mandates zero-liability for customers when fraud occurs due to bank system compromise. Banks must provide 24-hour fraud reporting channel and reimburse within 10 working days. Proactive customer advisory when new malware targeting their app is detected.
key_controls: Zero customer liability for bank-side fraud
Proactive SMS/email advisory to affected customers
Reimburse within 10 working days pending investigation
Maintain fraud risk score for all mobile transactions
rbi_directives.customer_advisory_trigger: When APK targeting bank's package name is detected with Critical or High Risk score
rbi_directives.advisory_template: Alert: A malicious app impersonating banking services has been detected. Do NOT install apps from unofficial sources. If you have installed a suspicious app, please call our fraud helpline at 1800-XXX-XXXX immediately.

rbi_directives.title: RBI-CSITE Threat Intelligence Bulletin — Android Banking Malware 2023
rbi_directives.summary: RBI Computer Science and Information Technology Examination (RBI-CSITE) advisory on rise of Android banking trojans targeting Indian banking apps. Drinik, Xenomorph, and SOVA variants specifically targeting Indian UPI ecosystem.
active_threats: Drinik v3 — targets SBI, BOI, PNB with accessibility overlay
Xenomorph — targeting 400+ banking apps globally including Indian banks
SOVA — sophisticated banking trojan with 2FA bypass
FluBot — SMS worm with OTP interception, Indian campaigns active
indicators: UPI deep links in malicious APKs

cert_in_advisories.title: Android Dropper Malware — Banking Sector Alert
cert_in_advisories.summary: CERT-In has observed a campaign distributing Android dropper APKs through WhatsApp and SMS links. The droppers use DexClassLoader to fetch second-stage banking trojan payloads from pastebin-like services.
iocs: Package names resembling IRCTC, Income Tax, or bank apps
recommended_actions: Block ngrok.io and pastebin.com at network perimeter
Alert customers not to install APKs from WhatsApp links
Deploy mobile threat defense on corporate devices
Report compromised devices to CERT-In within 6 hours

cert_in_advisories.title: Drinik Banking Trojan — New Variant Alert
cert_in_advisories.summary: New Drinik variant targeting 18 Indian banks with updated overlay screens for UPI PIN harvesting. Uses Accessibility Service to auto-fill OTPs and perform fraudulent transfers.
iocs: SHA256 families: known Drinik hash clusters
recommended_actions: Block identified package names on MDM
Monitor for transactions from devices with Accessibility abuse
Enable behavioral analytics for UPI transactions

npci_security.title: UPI Ecosystem Security — Mobile App Requirements
npci_security.summary: NPCI mandates all UPI apps to implement root detection, emulator detection, overlay attack prevention, and secure keyboard for PIN entry. Apps must detect when another app is drawing over them during payment flow.
requirements: Overlay attack detection during PIN entry
Network security config with certificate pinning

indian_banking_packages.com.boi: Bank of India — BHIM BOI UPI
indian_banking_packages.com.sbi.lotusintouch: State Bank of India — YONO SBI
